title: Ptyssiglottis collina
taxonomy: Ptyssiglottis collina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, a group of plants commonly known as the African violet or Gesneriad family. The genus Ptyssiglottis is native to Southeast Asia, and the species Ptyssiglottis collina is also found in this region. This plant is a member of the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes mint, lavender, and snapdragon families. Overall, the classification of Ptyssiglottis collina is a reflection of its evolutionary history and its relationships to other plants within the Gesneriaceae family.

intro: Ptyssiglottis collina, also known as the Chinese catmint or cat grass, is a type of plant species in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
